title: Permutations
description: Given an array nums of distinct integers, return all possible permutations. You can return the answer in any order.
hints: Use a recursive backtracking approach.
Swap elements in-place to generate permutations.
Track which elements have been used with a boolean array.

title: Permutations II
description: Given a collection of numbers nums that might contain duplicates, return all possible unique permutations in any order.
hints: Sort the array first to handle duplicates easily.
Skip duplicate elements in the same recursive level.
Use a boolean array to track used elements.

title: Subsets
description: Given an integer array nums of unique elements, return all possible subsets (the power set). The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets. Return the solution in any order.
hints: Use backtracking to include or exclude each element.
At each step, you can choose to add the current element or skip it.
The recursion tree has 2^n leaves.

title: Subsets II
description: Given an integer array nums that may contain duplicates, return all possible subsets (the power set). The solution set must not contain duplicate subsets.
hints: Sort the array first to group duplicates together.
Skip duplicates at the same recursive level.
Include the current element and skip the rest of the duplicates.

title: Combination Sum
description: Given an array of distinct integers candidates and a target integer target, return a list of all unique combinations of candidates where the chosen numbers sum to target. The same number may be chosen from candidates an unlimited number of times.
hints: Sort candidates to enable early termination.
Use backtracking with a remaining target value.
Allow reuse of the same element by not advancing the index.

title: Combination Sum II
description: Given a collection of candidate numbers candidates (with duplicates) and a target number target, find all unique combinations in candidates where the candidate numbers sum to target. Each number in candidates may only be used once in the combination.
hints: Sort the array to handle duplicates.
Skip duplicate elements at the same recursive level.
Each element can only be used once, so advance the index.

title: N-Queens
description: The n-queens puzzle is the problem of placing n queens on an n x n chessboard such that no two queens attack each other. Given an integer n, return the number of distinct solutions to the n-queens puzzle.
hints: Use backtracking to place queens row by row.
Track columns, diagonals, and anti-diagonals to check validity.
Use sets for O(1) conflict checking.

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def solveNQueens(self, n: int) -> List[List[str]]:
        res = []
        board = [['.'] * n for _ in range(n)]
        cols = set()
        diag1 = set()  # row - col
        diag2 = set()  # row + col
        def backtrack(row):
            if row == n:
                res.append([''.join(r) for r in board])
                return
            for col in range(n):
                if col in cols or (row - col) in diag1 or (row + col) in diag2:
                    continue
                cols.add(col)
                diag1.add(row - col)
                diag2.add(row + col)
                board[row][col] = 'Q'
                backtrack(row + 1)
                board[row][col] = '.'
                cols.remove(col)
                diag1.remove(row - col)
                diag2.remove(row + col)
        backtrack(0)
        return res

title: Word Search
description: Given an m x n grid of characters board and a string word, return true if word exists in the grid. The word can be constructed from letters of sequentially adjacent cells (horizontally or vertically). The same cell may not be used more than once.
hints: Try DFS from every cell that matches the first character.
Mark visited cells temporarily and restore them after recursion.
Early termination: if no matching cells remain, return false.

title: Generate Parentheses
description: Given n pairs of parentheses, write a function to generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.
hints: Use backtracking with counts of open and close parentheses.
Only add '(' if open < n, and ')' if close < open.
A valid sequence has open == close == n at the end.

title: Sudoku Solver
description: Write a program to solve a Sudoku puzzle by filling the empty cells. A sudoku solution must satisfy all of the following rules: Each of the digits 1-9 must occur exactly once in each row, each column, and each of the nine 3x3 sub-boxes. Empty cells are indicated by the '.' character.
hints: Use backtracking: try placing digits 1-9 in empty cells.
Use hash sets to track which digits are already used in rows, columns, and boxes.
Return immediately when a valid solution is found.
